faculty_name: Faculty of Applied Sciences and Technology
note: The Faculty of Applied Sciences and Technology houses the Departments of Statistical Sciences, Hospitality and Tourism Management, Computer Science, Food Sciences and Technology, and Actuarial Sciences.
Offers PhD, MTech, MSc, BTech, and BSc programmes in statistics, IT, AI, cybersecurity, data science, food science, hospitality, tourism, media, and actuarial science.

courses.course_name: MTech Hospitality and Tourism Management (Travel and Tourism Management option; Catering and Hospitality Management option)

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year
mode: Weekend mode: Lectures on Fridays and Saturdays
entry_requirements: A good first degree (Second Class Lower or better, CGPA ≥ 2.5) in Hospitality, Tourism, or related field from an accredited university
Third Class holders with at least 4 years relevant working experience may be considered
Must pass a selection interview
level_note: Two specialisation options: Travel and Tourism Management; Catering and Hospitality Management.
Research track includes taught courses and a research thesis.

mode: Weekend mode: Lectures on Fridays and Saturdays
entry_requirements: Same as Research track entry requirements
Non-Research track is coursework-based and completed in one year
level_note: Non-Research (taught) track for professionals seeking advanced knowledge without thesis requirement.
Covers same specialisation options as the Research track.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year
entry_requirements: A good first degree (Second Class Lower or better, CGPA ≥ 2.5) in Sports Science, Business, Marketing, or related field
Third Class holders with 4+ years relevant experience may be considered
level_note: Research programme in sports management and marketing.
Addresses Ghana's growing commercialisation of sports and athletics.

entry_requirements: Same as MTech Sports Management (Research) entry requirements
level_note: Taught master's for sports industry professionals.
Capstone project replaces thesis.

courses.course_name: MTech Event, Recreation and Leisure Management (Research)

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year
entry_requirements: A good first degree (Second Class Lower or better, CGPA ≥ 2.5) in Hospitality, Tourism, Events, Business, or related field
Third Class holders with 4+ years experience may apply
level_note: Research programme exploring event design, recreation, and leisure economics.
Thesis-based with taught components in the first year.

entry_requirements: Same as MTech Event, Recreation and Leisure Management (Research) entry requirements
level_note: Taught master's for events and recreation professionals.
Capstone project replaces thesis.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year
entry_requirements: A good first degree (Second Class Lower or better, CGPA ≥ 2.5) in Computer Science, IT, Mathematics, Statistics, or related field
Third Class holders with 4+ years relevant experience may be considered

level_note: Cutting-edge programme addressing AI talent demand in Ghana and Africa.
Covers ML, deep learning, NLP, and computer vision with applied project components.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year

Third Class holders with 4+ years relevant experience may be considered

level_note: Addresses Ghana's growing cybersecurity skills gap in banking, telecoms, and government.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 12,000 - 18,000 per year (based on comparable Technical University PhD rates)
entry_requirements: Master's degree (MTech/MSc/MPhil) in Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, or related quantitative field with minimum CGPA of 2.5 or equivalent from an accredited university
Must submit a 3,000-word research proposal and pass a selection interview
At least 2 years of relevant postgraduate experience preferred
level_note: TaTU's flagship doctoral programme and one of the few PhD offerings at a Ghanaian Technical University.
Focuses on computational and applied statistics research at the intersection of statistics, computing, and data science.

fees: Ghanaian Students (Regular): GHC 1,555 per semester (2022/2023 rates)
cutt_off: WASSCE aggregate: 24 or better; SSSCE aggregate: 36 or better
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English Language, Core Mathematics, and Integrated Science plus Credits in three (3) relevant science/tech elective subjects. Aggregate 24 or better.
SSSCE: Credits (A-D) in the same subjects. Aggregate 36 or better.
TVET Certificate II/Technician Part II: Relevant technical passes plus required core subjects.
Mature applicants: 25+ years with relevant work experience; must pass entrance exam and interview.
level_note: Core IT programme covering software, networks, and systems.
Graduates work in banks, telecoms, fintech, and government IT.

faculty_name: Faculty of Creative Arts and Technology
note: The Faculty of Creative Arts and Technology houses the Departments of Art and Design Innovation, Fashion and Design, Textiles Technology, and Graphic Communication Design Technology.
Offers MTech and BTech programmes in art and design, graphic communication, fashion, and textiles.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year
mode: Weekend mode with research thesis and studio practice
entry_requirements: A good first degree (Second Class Lower or better, CGPA ≥ 2.5) in Art, Design, Fashion, Industrial Art, or related creative field
Portfolio review may be required
Third Class holders with 4+ years experience may apply
level_note: Research programme in art and design theory and practice.
Thesis involves studio-based or theoretical research in art and design.

mode: Weekend mode, coursework with capstone studio project
entry_requirements: Same as MTech Art and Design (Research) entry requirements
level_note: Taught master's for practising designers and artists.
Capstone studio project replaces thesis.

note: The Faculty of Agriculture and Natural Resources houses the Departments of Sustainable Agriculture, Natural Resource Management, and Agribusiness.
Offers MTech and BTech programmes in crop science, sustainable agriculture, agribusiness, and natural resource management.

fees: Ghanaian Students: Estimated GHC 7,000 - 10,000 per year

level_note: Advanced crop science programme for agricultural professionals and researchers.
Research thesis focuses on crop production, protection, or post-harvest technology in northern Ghana and beyond.
